comment: Restore-then-replay bit-identity for a role filled INSIDE advance_hours (DESIGN_QUESTS §4/§10 — the determinism trap is armed on this surface on fork engines). `hauler` fills on the `crossed` stage, which latches on a `since` gate (2 days after the root) — a clock-RELATIVE gate so the test is robust to the harness's monotonic accumulated day, exactly like hook_replay. replay_advance runs the same span TWICE from a restored snapshot and asserts journal.*/choice.*/test.* are byte-identical: the fill happens during the replayed catch-up, and the binding journal.role_replay.role.hauler rides journal.* into the fingerprint. A candidate enumeration that leaned on Dictionary insertion order (not the id sort) would diverge here; reed sorts before wren with no prefer, so it fills reed identically every run.

stages.journal: Word came that someone would carry the water once the ground dried.
$hauler came on the second day, as the pools cracked open.
